title: Moist Saudi-arabian Onion Tagine
description: This savory dish from Saudi Arabia showcases the rich flavor of onions, cheese, and spices, perfect for a solo dinner. Cooked to perfection using a sous-vide for an ultra-tender result.
instructions: Heat the butter and olive oil in a large saucepan over medium heat.
Add the sliced onions and cook, stirring occasionally, until caramelized and dark brown, about 30 minutes.
Add the garam masala, cumin, paprika, salt, and black pepper, and stir to combine.
Add the shredded mozzarella cheese and stir until melted and gooey.
Transfer the tagine to a sous-vide water bath at 1604F (71C) and cook for 45 minutes.
Remove the tagine from the sous-vide and garnish with chopped parsley.
Let the tagine rest for a few minutes before serving to allow the cheese to set.
This will help create the signature cheese pull effect.
Be patient and enjoy the process!
Note: For an extra crispy top, broil for an additional 2-3 minutes before serving.
